nonBlockingIssues.description: SkillMarkdown.tsx uses var(--line-height-relaxed) (1.5) instead of var(--line-height-loose) (1.6) for line heights. The original hardcoded value was 1.6, so --line-height-loose would be the semantically correct replacement.

The border-subtle utility is used with opacity modifiers (e.g., border-subtle/80) in ChatMessageList.tsx. The @utility definition sets border-color directly, which may not support Tailwind's opacity modifier syntax. However, build passes and visual correctness should be verified during user testing.
Domain components GraphStepNode.tsx and ArtifactPreview.tsx still contain border border-border-subtle patterns that could be updated for consistency.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that typography configuration files (like lib/config/typo.ts) should be considered alongside inline styles when migrating hardcoded values to CSS variables for complete consistency.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature replace-inline-styles replaced inline styles in components but left hardcoded line-height values in lib/config/typo.ts untouched. This creates inconsistency where CSS variables are used in some places but hardcoded values in centralized typography presets.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit mention of typography configuration files as potential targets for CSS variable migration, alongside component inline styles.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The styling-worker skill focused on inline styles but didn't mention configuration files like typo.ts that define centralized presets used across the codebase.

VAL-STYLE-001.evidence: No hardcoded hex/rgb colors found in component files

VAL-STYLE-002.evidence: Inline styles minimized - remaining styles use CSS variables or acceptable CSS keywords

VAL-STYLE-003.evidence: max-w-content and border-subtle utilities available in tailwind.css

VAL-STYLE-004.evidence: Empty fonts.css removed; theme.css is single source of truth
